svg.text: Reinforcement-learning Quake 2 bot: training harness, custom engine bridge, live human-vs-bot deployment
n_servers=12 × n_bots_per_server=8 (n_ml_bots=4/server → 48 concurrent ML bot slots)
— CurriculumEvolver: retires mastered maps, seeds fresh mlcur_* replacements live
spawns/owns one q2ded process per server; 1 UDP socket per ML bot slot
— Observation/Action struct packing (matches C ml_bridge.h wire format)
per-256u-cell session memory (engagement/threat/opportunity/self-fire), keyed by exact map name;
reward-shapes training AND feeds 21 of the policy's 24 "session memory" obs dims
[C] merge_mod/lithium (Lithium II Mod v1.32-beta2, compiled → game.so)
G_RunFrame — two-phase lockstep: sends every ML bot's obs first, then runs entities
Bot_Think — dispatches ML bridge / 3ZB2 legacy AI / dead-respawn per entity
ML_PackObs, ML_ApplyAction (act → ent->velocity), ML_UpdateStuckGuard (anti-stuck safety net)
ML_SendObsOnly / ML_RecvAction — the UDP send/receive bridge itself
SV_Physics_Step / SV_FlyMove — velocity → position integration, collision
48 ML bot slots (across 12 servers) share this same source — batched multi-bot timing
gives ML_RecvAction's bootstrap check enough incidental slack to succeed (see finding #1 below)
3zb2/ botlist configs required even for pure-ML training — silent empty-spawn otherwise
— ONNX Runtime, CPU-only (~66MB RSS vs ~2.1GB for torch+CUDA)
— background bsp/vis/rad compile of a fresh map every round
sv_downloadserver → HTTP map delivery (was: slow legacy UDP transfer)
deterministic decode: cont_mean directly, argmax for jump/fire/hook/weapon
num_ml_bots=1 — solo bot, no 3ZB2 fill opponents by default
identical class to training — same protocol.py, same spatial.py VoxelSpatialReward
non-blocking MSG_DONTWAIT check never escaped for a solo bot → EVERY action applied as zero, forever ("stuck at spawn")
same g_main.c / func.c / ml_obs.c / ml_bridge.c / g_phys.c — only ml_bridge.c's
bootstrap timeout differs from the pre-fix build (50ms real wait vs 0ms MSG_DONTWAIT)
solo deployment has none of training's 48-bot incidental scheduling slack — this is why the bug only bit here
same custom build, public UDP :28000, Q2_BIND_IP="" (bind-all workaround)
admin.lst wildcard admin cred set; 3zb2/ present but unused (solo config)
custom yquake2 build (bot-compat-patches — MUST match server's MAX_MSGLEN=8192)
python http.server :32494 — maps + client tarball + install.sh
15:1 kill/death internal target NOT confirmed passed
✓ FIXED — "stuck at spawn": ml_bridge.c ML_RecvAction() bootstrap race condition
The bootstrap path (meant to handle only the very first frame, before Python starts listening) checked for a reply with a non-blocking, zero-wait recv.
Escaping it required Python's reply to already be buffered at the exact instant checked — training's 48-bot batched timing gave enough incidental slack for that
race to resolve; a solo live bot never had it, so every action applied as the zero-initialized fallback, forever. Fixed with a bounded 50ms real timeout; deployed to production.
✗ OPEN — aim/yaw-tracking of visible targets is essentially non-functional
Measured across 318 frames with a visible enemy (two live test runs, post-fix): mean facing error 97.5° (median 97.8°). Only 6% of frames were within the
training reward's own 12° alignment threshold (Q2_AIM_YAW_DEG). Of 40 recorded fire events, zero happened while aligned within that threshold. Movement
and target-detection clearly work (distance/contact modulate behavior) — specifically the yaw-to-target coupling does not, independent of the bootstrap fix.
△ PROTOTYPE — old checkpoint's lattice pulls are weak/inverted; direction objective added
During pure exploration (no visible enemy, so the lattice is the only spatial signal): "opportunity" pull (steer.py's "value/target is here") correlates weakly
positively with movement (cos-sim ≈ +0.11, n=75). "Engagement" pull ("expect combat here") correlates negatively (cos-sim ≈ −0.49, n=75) — the bot tends to
move AWAY from engagement history. PPO now adds a Quake-basis direction objective outside visible combat; a newly-trained checkpoint must prove the inversion fixed.
✓ PROTOTYPE — generator priors, route/item readiness, and lattice persistence are wired up
Compile installs .lattice/.routes sidecars; spatial.py preloads objective/danger cells and projects item clocks into opportunity/threat. Learned cells save beside policy
checkpoints. Own pickups re-phase timers; enemy-take inference still awaits engine item-visibility spawn IDs.
Sources: this investigation session (2026-07-09/11), q2-ml-bot + merge_mod/lithium repos (canonical: nobara-pc ~/Documents/Cascade_Projects/yquake, git-tracked).
Training box (WSL/RTX2080) runs a non-git snapshot of the same source, confirmed byte-identical on all files discussed above.
